business_description.nature_of_business: Hospitality operations including hotels and restaurants with clerical office staff

coverages_discussed.notes: Policy Number WC-HCHP-2025-3350; Large Risk rating plan; Interstate Rating; Final Experience Modification Factor 0.94

positives: Final Experience Modification Factor of 0.94 (credit mod, below 1.00)
Actual primary losses ($92,945) significantly below expected losses ($361,806)
No excess losses reported across three policy years
Declining claim frequency trend (8 claims in 2021-2022, 6 in 2022-2023, 2 in 2023-2024)
concerns: Multiple claims reported each policy year across the three-year experience period
Hospitality industry exposure including bellhops/doorpersons (higher hazard class 9060)
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Three-year loss experience: 2021-2022 had 8 claims totaling $56,343; 2022-2023 had 6 claims totaling $26,662; 2023-2024 had 2 claims totaling $9,940. All losses were primary with zero excess losses. Total actual primary losses of $92,945 versus expected losses of $361,806.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: Texas Department of Insurance Experience Rating Worksheet for Hill Country Hospitality Partners, LLC. Policy WC-HCHP-2025-3350 effective 2025-05-01 to 2026-05-01, valuation date 2026-03-15. Large Risk rating plan with interstate rating applied. Risk ID 308382-9453.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Hospitality operations classified under TX class codes: 9052 Hotel — All Other Employees (payroll $9,207,271); 9060 Hotel — Bellhops, Doorpersons (payroll $1,454,400); 9082 Restaurant — NOC (payroll $3,678,800); 8810 Clerical Office Employees — NOC (payroll $1,920,000).
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: Rating calculation: Expected Losses (E) $361,806; Actual Primary Losses (Ap) $92,945; Actual Excess Losses (Ae) $0; Weighting Factor (W) 0.419; Expected Excess Losses (Ee) $422,385; Ballast Value (B) $29,114. Numerator [Ap + W×Ae + (1-W)×Ee + B] = $367,465; Denominator [E + B] = $390,920; Calculated Mod 0.9400; Final Experience Modification Factor 0.94.
